description_long: This object is a traditional water well featuring a circular stone base constructed from large, gray stone blocks, some of which appear to be dislodged and scattered around the base. The well is topped with a pitched, terracotta-tiled roof supported by two vertical wooden posts, providing shelter from above. The stone wall is thick and uneven, with visible gaps and missing blocks, giving it a rustic, weathered appearance. The interior of the well is open and dark, suggesting significant depth, and there is no visible water-drawing mechanism such as a bucket or pulley. The roof tiles are arranged in a wavy, overlapping pattern, and the structure as a whole is utilitarian with no visible decorative elements. The well is designed to be a functional amenity, likely intended for public or communal use in an urban or park setting.
description: A rustic stone water well with a terracotta-tiled roof and scattered stone blocks, supported by wooden posts and lacking a visible water-drawing mechanism.
description_view_0: Front view showing the open well, two supporting wooden posts, and several stone blocks scattered at the base.
description_view_1: Side view highlighting the uneven stone wall, a single visible wooden post, and a few displaced stone blocks.
description_view_2: Rear angled view showing the roof's overhang, both wooden posts, and the open well interior.
description_view_3: Back view with a clear look at the stone wall, one wooden post, and a single stone block on the ground.
attribute_surrounding_protective_structu: Circular stone wall approximately 1 meter high, made of large stone blocks; pitched terracotta-tiled roof supported by two wooden posts.
